concepts.label: Data Flow
concepts.summary: A description of how data moves between people, systems, or external parties.
concepts.why_it_exists: Data flows make privacy work concrete. They show where personal or sensitive data comes from, where it goes, and who touches it.
how_to_use: Register the main flow, source, destination, and purpose of the movement.
Keep the flow linked to the relevant processing activity when possible.

relations.summary: A data flow often explains one part of a processing activity.

concepts.label: Processing Activity
concepts.summary: A record of one business activity that processes data for a defined purpose.
concepts.why_it_exists: Processing activities help the organization explain why data is processed, by whom, and with what safeguards.
how_to_use: Register the purpose, context, and key handling details of the activity.
Use related data flows to make the activity understandable in practice.

relations.summary: Data flows can explain how the activity works in practice.
